diagrams.title: Memory Layout of Array of Objects
diagrams.description: Shows contiguous memory allocation for 3 Student objects, each containing roll and name
elements: Each object occupies fixed size based on data members
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how objects are stored in memory contiguously

diagrams.title: Constructor Invocation Sequence
diagrams.description: Shows when constructors are called during array creation
elements: Line 1: Student s[3]; triggers constructor 3 times
Destructors called in reverse order when array ends
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing object lifecycle in array of objects

diagrams.title: Accessing Members in Array
diagrams.description: Shows syntax for accessing object members using index
elements: s[0].display() - calls display() on object at index 0
s[1].getRoll() - returns roll of object at index 1
s[2].setMarks(95) - sets marks for object at index 2
diagrams.purpose: Remembering correct syntax for object member access

diagrams.title: Stack vs Heap Allocation
diagrams.description: Compares stack and heap array of objects
elements: Stack: Student s[5]; - auto cleanup, limited size
Heap: Student* p = new Student[5]; - manual, larger
delete[] p; required for heap to call destructors
